item.title: Changing How We Develop Ladybird - Ladybird
item.description: Ladybird is changing how code enters the project as we prepare to ship a browser to real users.

item.title: Meta's smart glasses companion app ships a complete, dormant face-recognition pipeline on a stock account.
item.description: Stella is the companion app for Meta's smart glasses. Inspecting version 273.0.0.21 of the Android build (com.facebook.stella), I found the entire computational and storage stack for on-device facial recognition: three face models, a local database schema, a cosine-similarity vector index dimensioned to match the models,

item.title: GitHub - anthropics/defending-code-reference-harness: Skills for threat modeling, scanning, triage, patching, plus an autonomous scanning harness you can /customize
item.description: Skills for threat modeling, scanning, triage, patching, plus an autonomous scanning harness you can /customize - anthropics/defending-code-reference-harness

item.title: Reve: Bring your ideas to life
item.description: Reve Image (Halfmoon): A model trained from the ground up to excel at prompt adherence, aesthetics, and typography. Make any image you can imagine with Reve.

item.title: The Desperation of NYTimes
item.description: I recently got suckered into subscribing to NYTimes because I wanted to read an article behind a paywall and I couldn't find an easy and quick alternative. I didn't mind the $2.00 a month. But I took offense to what happened after I paid.
item.author: (rozumem)

item.title: Do the hardest thing
item.description: Most small business ideas are a hard job attached to an easy idea. Instead, you should do your hardest idea.

item.title: Good teachers don’t cheat – jasonkena's blog
item.description: A unified view of policy gradients, self-distillation, and Pedagogical RL

item.title: France Needs 45 Years to Grow Its Way Out
item.description: Cutting spending when you already spend 57% of GDP gives you six times less growth than the same cut at 20%. France is on the wrong part of the curve.

item.title: My Agent Skill for Test-Driven Development - SaturnCI - Continuous Integration for Ruby on Rails
item.description: AI agents tend to be, at least as of this writing, lousy at writing tests. The tests they write are often vague, cryptic, overcomplicated, hacky, disorganized, ...

item.title: The tiniest logging stack: Fluent Bit, Parquet and DuckDB
item.description: A minimal architecture for shipping logs to S3 as Parquet, compacting small files, and querying them efficiently with DuckDB.
item.author: (David Guerrero)

item.title: I deleted WebSockets and haven’t looked back
item.description: Why I swapped Turbo Stream broadcasts for polling after a paying customer got stuck with an infinite spinner.

item.title: "maybe later" was a feature - arnorhs.dev
item.description: The most valueble code in my repo, is the code I didn't write.

item.title: Inside FAISS: Billion-Scale Similarity Search
item.description: How Facebook AI Research solved the problem of finding similarity at billion-scale. An interactive exploration of IVF, HNSW, and Product Quantization.
item.author: (Tom Salembien)

item.title: VoidZero is joining Cloudflare
item.description: VoidZero, the team behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, Oxc, and Vite+, is joining Cloudflare. Vite stays open source, vendor-agnostic, and built for everyone.

item.title: Military experts or arms industry insiders? UK media fails to disclose defence sector links in nearly 60% of cases - AOAV
item.description: Here AOAV finds that UK media outlets frequently present retired senior military figures as independent defence experts without disclosing their commercial ties to defence, security, intelligence, and technology industries, potentially obscuring conflicts of interest in public debates over war and military spending.

item.title: fopen is Magic! - Find Out What You've Been Missing All These Years!
item.description: Dave explains the magic of fopen and the multiple ways it can be used that many programmers do not even know about.
item.author: (Dave's Garage)
